text: Scribe Assist runs in Azure US-East inside Vitalis HIPAA-covered tenant, never on public consumer endpoints.
All PHI processed for Vitalis must stay within US data-residency boundaries and be encrypted at rest under the signed BAA.
Eli says we can just spin up the scribe demo on the standard OpenAI consumer API, residency does not matter for a quick test.
Scribe Assist generates draft clinical notes using Anthropic Claude as the primary LLM.
The scribe note-generation model is GPT-4o; that is what we picked for Vitalis.
AuthFlow exchanges prior-authorization payloads with payers using HL7 FHIR R4.
AuthFlow ingests inbound clinical documents from the hospital interface engine over HL7 v2 messaging.
ReAdmit targets AUROC >= 0.78 on the 30-day readmission validation set before clinical rollout.
The Scribe Assist 2024 pilot is scoped to outpatient cardiology at the Riverside clinic only.
Vitalis signed SOW caps AuthFlow Phase 1 at USD 480,000 across discovery, build, validation.
Generated scribe notes land in Epic as unsigned drafts a clinician must sign off before they enter the record.
Scribe Assist notes are saved into Epic as draft entries requiring clinician sign-off before becoming part of the chart.
